title: Privilege Escalation via Sudo Abuse
story: CRITICAL: An unprivileged web application service account has been observed
executing commands as root via sudo.
The account 'webuser' does not have sudo privileges in policy, yet root-level
commands have appeared in audit logs. This indicates either a misconfiguration
being exploited or a privilege escalation attack in progress.
Identify the escalation chain and determine what actions were taken as root.

objectives.label: Identify the sudo binary/command used to escalate
What command was run as root?
What was the original entry vector (how did webuser get a shell)?

hints: Look for sudo events in the auth logs: 'COMMAND' entries in sudolog
The escalation binary is a legitimate system tool often found in GTFOBins
Check what happened before the sudo event — how was the shell obtained?
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case5_* webuser | sort _time | table _time, sourcetype, _raw
